stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 30.0 °C and a standard deviation of 3.3 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.9% and 4.5%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 30.0 °C. After correcting for the 3.2% bias: ≈ 29.1 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.9%-4.5%) and sampling variability: [27.4, 30.7]. Point estimate: 29.1 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
